sections.title: Night Records
sections.what: Reza retrieves archived charts from his intern year, alone after shift. He's reconstructing the near-miss methodically, driven by probation and a suspicion he can't yet name.

sections.title: Agnes at the Threshold
sections.what: Agnes watches the ER from the chapel doorway, reading stress patterns in the unit. She notes Harte circling Theo's station more than usual tonight.

sections.title: Performance Review, Informal
sections.what: Harte corners Theo in the admin corridor, framing the meeting as managerial concern. The subtext is unmistakable: stop asking questions about the diverted ward.

sections.title: The Wrong Handwriting
sections.what: Reza finds a chart entry in unfamiliar handwriting dated the night of his near-miss. The entry exonerates him and implicates someone else entirely.

sections.title: What Agnes Knows
sections.what: Theo slips into the chapel after Harte's ambush. Agnes already knows what was said; she tells him Harte's pressure follows a pattern she has seen before.

sections.title: The Board Member's Name
sections.what: Reza matches the handwriting to a current staff list. The name belongs to a board member — the attending who supervised his intern year.

sections.title: First Approach
sections.what: Reza engineers a casual encounter with the board member in the admin corridor. He floats an oblique question; the response tells him the man knows exactly who Reza is.

sections.title: Agnes and the Shape of the Building
sections.what: Agnes tells Theo that secrets don't stay buried — they migrate to whoever the institution can least afford to lose. She means him specifically.

sections.title: What the Chart Means Now
sections.what: Reza sits alone with the chart, deciding what first approach actually costs when the other person has thirty years of institutional armor.
